summary_text: { "scores": { "accuracy": 0, "performance": 2, "maintainability": 1 }, "total": 3, "issues": [ { "severity": "critical", "file": "scripts/fugue-execute.mjs", "line": "155-168, 230, 374, 386, 400, 408", "description": "Run state persistence leaks unredacted child process output into `~/.claude/state/runs/*.json` and `phase-evidence.jsonl`. `runBridge()` returns raw `stdout`/`stderr`, `saveState()` writes the whole object, and `recordPhase()` logs summarized details without any secret filtering. This bypasses the redaction already present in `fugue-lane-bridge.mjs` and can persist tokens, secrets, or sensitive code/test output to disk.", "suggestion": "Persist only redacted summaries and artifact paths, not raw `stdout`/`stderr`. Apply the same secret-redaction path used by `fugue-lane-bridge.mjs` before any `saveState()` or `recordPhase()` write. If full outputs are needed, keep them only in the already-redacted lane output artifacts." }, { "severity": "critical", "file": "scripts/fugue-execute.mjs", "line": "506-509", "description": "Fresh-run failure handling breaks the core `--resume/--status` contract. In the catch path, non-resume executions generate a new random `runId` instead of updating the active run file, so the original run stays in a stale phase while a different blocked run is created. After an initial failure, `--status <original-id>` and `--resume <original-id>` can report or continue the wrong state.", "suggestion": "Track the active run ID in outer scope from initialization onward and always reuse that ID in the catch path. Update the existing run record atomically instead of synthesizing a new blocked run on failure." }, { "severity": "critical", "file": "scripts/fugue-execute.mjs", "line": "233-242", "description": "Consensus gating is weakened by hardcoding `-l 2 --claude-vote APPROVE` for every vote. This overrides the risk-based self-assessment in `consensus-vote.js` and always injects one approval vote even for high-risk/security-sensitive operations, materially reducing the chance of a `BLOCKED` outcome when it should happen.", "suggestion": "Pass the actual evaluated risk level for the current phase and let `consensus-vote.js` derive the Claude vote, or compute a real vote from task/risk metadata instead of forcing `APPROVE`." }, { "severity": "critical", "file": "scripts/fugue-execute.mjs", "line": "352-366", "description": "The declared done criteria are not actually validating the target implementation. The syntax gate checks only `scripts/fugue-execute.mjs` itself, not the generated/modified project code in `runState.project`. This allows runs to be marked `done` even when the implementation under review has syntax errors, as long as tests do not catch them.", "suggestion": "Run syntax/type validation against the target project or changed artifacts, not the orchestrator file. If the project has no standard syntax script, derive checks from package scripts (`lint`, `typecheck`, `build`) or explicitly track changed files and validate those." } ], "summary": "Assumption: I interpreted the 9-step flow from the phases encoded in `fugue-execute.mjs` because no separate 9-step spec was found in this directory. The CLI flags, run-state directory, spark routing, and `consensus-vote.js` integration are present, but the implementation is not safe to approve: state persistence leaks sensitive output, failure-state resumability is broken on fresh runs, vote risk handling is biased toward approval, and the done criteria can mark broken project code as complete. Non-critical checklist misses also exist (`scripts/fugue-execute.mjs` is 516 lines, exceeding the stated 500-line limit), but I omitted them per the request to report critical issues only." }
